title: NOC 42101 – Firefighters | GTR Immigration
description: Firefighters under NOC 42101 are front-line emergency response professionals responsible for protecting lives, property, and the environment from fires, accidents, and disasters. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation requires specialized training, physical fitness, and certification depending on the province of employment. Firefighters work in municipal fire departments, provincial and federal emergency services, airports, industrial facilities, and specialized rescue units.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group42 – Front-line public protection services and paraprofessional occupationsSub-Major Group421 – Occupations in front-line public protection servicesMinor Group4210 – Occupations in front-line public protection servicesNOC Code42101

duties: Respond to fire alarms and emergency calls
Rescue victims from burning buildings and accident sites
Control and extinguish fires using hoses, ladders, and hydraulic tools
Administer first aid and emergency medical assistance
Educate the public on fire prevention
Train regularly to maintain physical fitness
Provide paramedical aid as part of trauma response teams
Conduct building inspections for fire code compliance
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program in:Fire protection technologyFire scienceRelated discipline
Firefighting and emergency medical training programs
Compulsory in: Nova Scotia, Quebec, Ontario, Manitoba, Alberta
Voluntary in: Newfoundland and Labrador, Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, Saskatchewan, British Columbia, Yukon, Northwest Territories, Nunavut
Experience as a volunteer firefighter may be an advantage.Several years of experience required for senior positions such as captain or lieutenant.
salary_prospects: $49,200 – $107,800 per year,depending on rank, experience, and province.
Senior officers and specialized unit members may earn higher compensation.
job_outlook: Expansion of emergency medical response roles
Fire services remain a critical public protection occupation in Canada.
labour_market_demand: Opportunities for promotion to captain or lieutenant
Specialized roles in emergency medical services or fire inspection
Fire services across Canada continue to recruit qualified candidates, especially in expanding municipalities.
pathways: Firefighters fall under TEER 2 skilled occupations, which may qualify for:
Express Entry (if eligibility criteria are met)
However, recruitment policies and citizenship requirements may apply depending on province and department.
If you are planning to pursue a firefighting career in Canada, contact GTR Immigration today to explore your immigration pathway.
